context: In Spanish every noun is either masculine or feminine, and the word for "the" changes to match: el for masculine, la for feminine. It is not about meaning - a table is not really "female" - it is just a label every noun carries. Get the article right and the rest of your Spanish starts to click.
explanation: Use el before a masculine noun and la before a feminine one. A good first rule: nouns ending in -o are usually masculine, and nouns ending in -a are usually feminine. It is only a tendency, not a law - el cafe and el pan are masculine even though they do not end in -o - so learn each noun together with its el or la. For "a / an" the same split gives un (masculine) and una (feminine).

examples.note: Masculine, even though it does not end in -o.
